C[DavidMitchell: K16 is too close to White's wall - G17 stops White playing a good extension and attack at F17 while defening the corner.  A White move at J17 is cramped so White will have to play elsewhere.
DavidMitchell: Good - jumping towards the centre when put under pressure is the right thing to do.
DavidMitchell: White R12 stops you connecting along the edge and aims at connecting under at R10.
DavidMitchell: S10 stops that connection - it also gives Black two options...
DavidMitchell: Black A connects to the corner - not an urgent thing but a parachute should Black get into trouble.
DavidMitchell: Black 'B' starts to made a base for Black while taking White's eye space.
DavidMitchell: P7 is a good move - it is the weak point of the knight's move.
DavidMitchell: Black at Q7 us a much simpler solution for Black and puts White in a difficult situation
DavidMitchell: White can capture two black stones by playing Q11 but White's two stones on the lower site are in great danger.
DavidMitchell: This move threaten a ladder (Q9).if White defends Black can protect the cut at O11 by playing O13.  White has to answer
DavidMitchell: Now White is in terrible trouble
DavidMitchell: White has pushed too much and is ignoring his own weaknesses - this is the time to benefit from that lapse
DavidMitchell: The sequence is still there...
DavidMitchell: White has to be very careful...
DavidMitchell: White's three cutting stones are in trouble and White's eye space has gone.
DavidMitchell: J10 the first truely bad move from Black
DavidMitchell: This move kills half of White's stones and rescues Black's group

DavidMitchell: First, you played very well at the beginning

DavidMitchell: O 10 is OK but you should play at O4 or O3
DavidMitchell: White will do this and you will feel afraid, but don't - you are strong and White is weak
